figures.caption: FIG. 4. Dilepton production rate as a function of invariant mass M . Here, the transverse momentum has been considered in the range 0.5 ‚â§ pT ‚â§ 2 GeV. Here, four temperature profiles have also been used to obtain the rates. (a) Case I: dissipative spin hydrodynamics with Œ∑/s0 = 5/4œÄ, œás = 10/4œÄ, dissipative hydrodynamics with Œ∑/s0 = 5/4œÄ, and (b) Case II: dissipative spin hydrodynamics with Œ∑/s0 = 1/4œÄ, œás = 3/4œÄ, dissipative standard hydrodynamics with Œ∑/s0 = 1/4œÄ. For the spin hydrodynamic case we consider CœâX(œÑ0) = CœâY (œÑ0) = CœâZ(œÑ0) = 80 MeV. We compare our results with the results obtained in Ref. [82] (brown dashed line) for a medium with non-vanishing vorticity œâ0 = 0.7 fm‚àí1 [82].

FIG. 3. Dilepton production rate as a function of transverse momenta pT . Here, the invariant mass is considered to be M = 0.4 GeV. For the estimation of the dilepton production rate the temperature profile of the medium has been obtained for (a) Case I: dissipative spin hydrodynamics with Œ∑/s0 = 5/4œÄ, œás = 10/4œÄ, dissipative hydrodynamics with Œ∑/s0 = 5/4œÄ, and (b) Case II: dissipative spin hydrodynamics with Œ∑/s0 = 1/4œÄ, œás = 3/4œÄ, dissipative hydrodynamics with Œ∑/s0 = 1/4œÄ. For the spin hydrodynamic case we consider CœâX(œÑ0) = CœâY (œÑ0) = CœâZ(œÑ0) = 80 MeV. We compare our results with the results obtained in Ref. [82]. The brown dashed line represents the dilepton rate from a medium with non-vanishing vorticity œâ0 = 0.7 fm‚àí1 obtained in Ref. [82].

FIG. 5. Dilepton production rate as a function of transverse momenta pT . Here, the invariant mass is considered to be M = 0.4 GeV. For this plot, T (œÑ ) has been obtained by solving spin hydrodynamic equations with Œ∑/s0 = 1/4œÄ, œás = 3/4œÄ. The red solid line corresponds to the case where CœâX(œÑ0) = CœâY (œÑ0) = 80 MeV, CœâZ(œÑ0) = 0. The green dashed line corresponds to CœâZ(œÑ0) = 80 MeV, CœâX(œÑ0) = CœâY (œÑ0) = 0.

FIG. 6. Dilepton production rate as a function of invariant mass M . Here, the transverse momentum has been considered in the range 0.5 ‚â§ pT ‚â§ 2 GeV. For this plot, T (œÑ ) has been obtained by solving spin hydrodynamic equations with Œ∑/s0 = 1/4œÄ, œás = 3/4œÄ.
